data.title: Bitcoin Mining Profitability Down 7.4% in March as Prices, Transaction Fees Fell: Jefferies
data.description:
• Bitcoin mining profitability fell 7.4% in March, according to Jefferies
  • The drop was due to a 11.2% decline in the average bitcoin price and a 9.1% slump in transaction fees, the report said.
  • Bitcoin thus far in April has outperformed the broader U.S. stock market, possibly due to the declining dollar, said the bank.

data.title: Nomura's Laser Digital Denies Involvement in Mantra Crash
data.description:
• Mantra's OM token lost 90% of its value shortly after $227 million of the token was sent to exchanges.
  • Despite early reports, Nomura Group's Laser Capital has denied sending any OM to exchanges.
  • Mantra's team claim the crash was a result of wider market pressures and a liquidation cascade.

data.title: Bitcoin Eyes Gains Versus Gold After Trendline Breakout, Monero Sees Golden Cross
data.description:
• Gold has outperformed bitcoin over the past 12 weeks, but technical charts suggest this trend may reverse.
  • BTC may catch up with the gold rally in the coming days.
  • Monero has shown a long-term bullish shift with a golden crossover, breaking out of a consolidation pattern.

data.title: Tether, Galaxy, Ledn Dominate CeFi Crypto Lending as DeFi Borrowing Soars, Research Shows
data.description:
• The crypto lending market stood at $36.5 billion in Q4 2024, still down 43% from its 2021 bull market peak, Galaxy reported.
  • DeFi borrowing grew 959% from the 2022 bear market bottom, reaching $19.1 billion across 20 protocols and 12 blockchains.
  • Tether, Galaxy, and Ledn hold nearly 90% of the $11.2 billion outstanding loans by centralized lenders.

data.title: Bitcoin ETFs Lose Over $800M in April as Institutions Stick With Bonds Amid Tariff Volatility
data.description:
• Despite social media calls to buy bitcoin, institutions are not following this trend, as evidenced by significant outflows from U.S.-listed spot BTC ETFs.
  • U.S. Treasury bills continue to attract strong institutional demand, highlighting their status as a safe haven amid economic uncertainty.
  • Rising U.S. recession odds and trade tensions have increased market volatility, affecting corporate earnings guidance and risk asset performance.

data.title: Binance, KuCoin, and Other Crypto Firms Hit by Amazon Web Service Issue
data.description:
• Binance and KuCoin temporarily suspended withdrawals due to issues with their data center provider, Amazon Web Services (AWS).
  • Binance resumed withdrawals shortly after the issue was reported, but users still face trading issues.
  • Other services like Rabby and DeBank also reported outages linked to AWS disruptions.

data.title: DEX KiloEx Loses $7M in Apparent Oracle Manipulation Attack
data.description:
• KiloEx, a decentralized exchange, suffered a $7 million loss due to a sophisticated attack exploiting a vulnerability in its price oracle system.
  • The attacker used Tornado Cash to fund a wallet and manipulated asset prices across multiple blockchain networks, including Base, BNB Chain, and Taiko.
  • KiloEx has suspended operations and is collaborating with partners to trace the stolen funds and blacklist the attacker's wallet.

data.title: Japanese Bonds Stir Unease as Bitcoin Recovers From Last Week's Tariff Panic
data.description:
• The yield on 30-year Japanese government bonds has surged to its highest level since 2004, raising concerns among investors.
  • That could incentivise Japanese funds to repatriate international investments, leading to another round of risk aversion in financial markets, including BTC.

data.title: Story’s IP Swings 20% in Volatile Trading Session
data.description:
• Story Protocol's IP tokens experienced a 20% drop and recovery within hours during an unusual trading session on Monday.
  • Trading volume surged on major exchanges Binance and OKX Spot, with $138 million recorded after the price rebound.
  • The sudden price movement was isolated from broader market trends, sparking speculation about insider activity or coordinated selling.

data.title: Dogecoin Slumps 3%, Bitcoin Steady Around $85K as Traders Fear U.S. Recession
data.description:
• Dogecoin fell 3% while Bitcoin and Ether remained stable amid easing tariff concerns and rising U.S. recession fears.
  • Prominent financial figures warn of a potential U.S. recession by 2025, with betting markets placing 40% to 60% odds.
  • Mantra's OM token surged 20% after a dramatic sell-off, while VeThor's VTHO rose 37% with UFC CEO Dana White joining as a strategic advisor.
